title: Go 1.26 Toolchain, Programs, Packages, and Evidence · Runtime Contract: Source Lexical Rules
summary: A small operations team needs a reproducible status tool, but its source, package boundary, formatter, build, runtime, and release identity are unclear. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go source lexical rules increment in a reproducible first-program and toolchain evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply UTF-8 source, identifiers, keywords, tokens, semicolon insertion, comments, and formatting rules without relying on visual coincidence.
Choose and interpret go run, build, install, test, fmt, vet, doc, env, version, and help evidence for a bounded task.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
